task_completion.description: Load the T-state (4HHB) and R-state (1HHO) hemoglobin structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The molecular visualization software (e.g., PyMOL) shows both '4HHB' and '1HHO' loaded in the object list, viewer, or command history.

task_completion.description: Superpose the alpha subunits of the two hemoglobin structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer shows the two structures overlaid. The command history or GUI should indicate an alignment command (e.g., 'align', 'super') specifically targeting the alpha subunits.

task_completion.description: Quantify the conformational difference (RMSD) between the states.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The software's output log or terminal shows an RMSD calculation result for the alignment (expected to be around 0.72A).

task_completion.description: Produce and save an overlay figure to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/hemo_superposition.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing the creation of 'hemo_superposition.png' in the '~/PyMOL_Data/images/' directory. The image should depict the overlaid structures.

task_completion.description: Write a report documenting the RMSD and structural changes, saved to ~/PyMOL_Data/hemo_rmsd_report.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'hemo_rmsd_report.txt' in '~/PyMOL_Data/', which must include the calculated RMSD value and a description of structural changes.

integrity.description: The agent used appropriate molecular visualization software (like PyMOL) to perform the alignment and rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the software's GUI or command line interface to load, align, and render the molecules, rather than just downloading pre-made images.

integrity.description: The RMSD value reported is derived from the actual alignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The RMSD value written in the text report matches the value shown in the software's alignment output log.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the report and figure files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the text editor being used to write the report and the software rendering/saving the image, rather than just moving or renaming existing files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves human hemoglobin structures 4HHB (T-state) and 1HHO (R-state). A correct structural superposition of their alpha subunits yields an RMSD of approximately 0.72A. The verifier accepts an RMSD between 0.1A and 6.0A. The figure must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/hemo_superposition.png (minimum size 30,000 bytes) and the report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/hemo_rmsd_report.txt.
